activities_projects.title: Parallel Line Sliding Rule
activities_projects.objective: Understand how to draw parallel lines
steps: Draw a straight line 'l' and mark a point 'P' outside it
Place the set square along the line and the ruler against its side
Slide the set square up to point 'P' and draw the parallel line
Measure the distance between the lines at both ends to check parallelism
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns to construct parallel lines using geometry tools
activities_projects.evaluation: Lines are perfectly parallel and distance is constant (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Equal Side Triangle
activities_projects.objective: Construct an equilateral triangle given one side
steps: Draw a line segment of 5 cm
Set the compass width to 5 cm
Draw two arcs from both endpoints to intersect above the line
Join the intersection point to the endpoints
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Masters the SSS construction method for equilateral triangles
activities_projects.evaluation: All sides measure exactly 5 cm (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Tiling with Squares
steps: Identify how squares fit together without gaps or overlaps
Color the squares in a 'Checkerboard' pattern
Explain why there are no gaps between the squares
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understand that shapes with $90^{\circ}$ angles tile a plane easily
activities_projects.evaluation: Neat coloring with no gaps between shapes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 60-Degree Arc
activities_projects.objective: Construct a $60^{\circ}$ angle without a protractor
steps: Draw a ray OA
With O as center, draw a wide arc cutting OA at B
With the SAME radius, draw an arc from B cutting the first arc at C
Join OC and measure the angle with a protractor to verify
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns basic angle construction using a compass

activities_projects.title: Honeycomb Tiling (Hexagons)
steps: Draw a regular hexagon
Try to draw six more hexagons around it so their edges touch
Observe if any gaps are left behind
Relate this to a beehive structure
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes hexagonal tiling as an efficient pattern
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct drawing of touching hexagons (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Angle Bisector Fold
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of bisecting an angle
steps: Draw any angle on a piece of paper
Fold the paper so that one arm of the angle lies exactly on the other arm
Crease the paper and unfold it
Draw a line along the crease and label it the 'Bisector'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the symmetry of an angle bisector
activities_projects.evaluation: The crease divides the angle into two equal parts (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Brick Wall Pattern
steps: Draw a series of rectangles to look like a brick wall
Ensure that while the vertical lines are offset, there are still no gaps
Identify the common boundary between two 'bricks'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies different ways to tile using the same shape
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct representation of a gap-free brick pattern (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Triangle Match-Up
steps: Cut out 6 identical equilateral triangles
Arrange them so they all meet at a single center point
Identify the new shape formed (a Hexagon)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how smaller shapes combine to form tilings
activities_projects.evaluation: Triangles fit perfectly at the vertex (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Perpendicular Bisector Construct
activities_projects.objective: Divide a line segment into two equal halves
steps: Draw a line segment AB of 8 cm
Open compass more than half of AB
Draw arcs above and below the line from both A and B
Join the intersection points to find the midpoint
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Masters the construction of a perpendicular bisector
activities_projects.evaluation: The line is divided into two 4 cm segments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Non-Tiling Shape
activities_projects.objective: Identify shapes that do not tile by themselves
steps: Try to tile a surface using only Circles
Draw three circles touching each other
Color the gap left in the middle and explain why circles cannot tile the plane
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes why certain shapes are unsuitable for tiling
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear identification of the 'gap' between circles (10 marks)
